title: artemisia campestris subsp. canadensis
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. canadensis, also known as Canadian wormwood, is a subspecies of the annual wormwood plant native to Canada and parts of the United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiparasitic agent. The health benefits of Canadian wormwood include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Canadian wormwood can be prepared for herbal use through tea infusions, tinctures, and essential oil extractions, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
